message:
feat(e2e,smoke): add failure signature classification and startup diagnostics to LD_PRELOAD smoke suite

Enhance the LD_PRELOAD smoke testing infrastructure with comprehensive
failure signature analysis, startup path tracking, and automated
troubleshooting documentation generation.

Failure signature system (ld_preload_smoke.sh):
- Add classify_failure_signature() that categorizes test failures into
  actionable signatures (startup_timeout, startup_segv, startup_abort,
  startup_symbol_lookup_error, etc.) based on exit codes and stderr
- Add signature_is_guarded() denylist check against
  FAILURE_SIGNATURE_DENYLIST env var — guarded signatures force test
  failure regardless of other pass criteria
- Add case_workload() and case_startup_path() helpers for per-case
  metadata extraction
- Wire signature/workload/startup_path through all trace emission,
  case recording, failure bundle, and report generation paths
- Add structured timing_json objects to all trace events
- Generate startup_troubleshooting.md with per-signature triage
  guidance, failing case summaries, and actionable investigation steps
- Make bead_id configurable via BEAD_ID env var (default: bd-1ah8)

E2E suite contract validation (check_e2e_suite.sh):
- Add Check 10: startup smoke diagnostics contract verification
  ensuring ld_preload_smoke.sh contains required signature markers
  (FAILURE_SIGNATURE_DENYLIST, signature_guard_triggered,
  classify_failure_signature, case_startup_path, etc.)

Test updates (e2e_suite_test.rs):
- Add failure signature field assertions to case schema validation
  (workload, startup_path, failure_signature, signature_guard_triggered)
- Relax bead_id assertion to accept bd-18qq.6 or bd-1ah8
- Add trace line schema validation for workload, startup_path,
  failure_signature, timing object, and signature_guard_triggered
- Replace hardcoded bead reference with BEAD_ID pattern

Beads:
- Update bd-1ah8 assignee to SwiftLark with timestamp refresh

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-19h","title":"Kernel: SOS barrier certificates (integrate into barrier/admissibility)","description":"Wire SOS barrier evaluation into RuntimeMathKernel decision law.

Plan:
- Feed barrier evaluator with state extracted from cached monitors (risk bound, quarantine depth, contention, etc.).
- If barrier indicates unsafe region: force FullValidate and/or Repair/Deny depending on mode.
- Export barrier margin in snapshot for observability.

Acceptance criteria:
- In strict mode, barrier only escalates (never silently repairs semantics).
- In hardened mode, barrier can trigger repair actions per policy.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleOwl","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:32:26.415903108Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T18:49:12.838748840Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T18:49:12.838730686Z","close_reason":"SOS barrier integrated into RuntimeMathKernel: Mutex field, cached_sos_barrier_state AtomicU8, observe wiring (provenance every call + quarantine on cadence), fusion vector index 60 (META_SEVERITY_LEN=36, SIGNALS=61), snapshot fields (provenance_value, quarantine_value, violations), decide() escalation at state>=2. 699 tests pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-19h","depends_on_id":"bd-19r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-19h","depends_on_id":"bd-2vf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-19j","title":"bd-z84 subtask: Futex mutex fast/slow path stabilization with deterministic state invariants","description":"Background:
- Existing mutex path still has hang/regression risk under LD_PRELOAD scenarios.

Goal:
- Implement and stabilize futex-backed mutex fast/slow paths with deterministic lock state invariants.

Deliverables:
1) Lock word layout + atomic transition protocol.
2) Fast path uncontended lock/unlock logic.
3) Slow path futex_wait/futex_wake with bounded retry/backoff policy.
4) Trylock semantics and cancellation-safe boundaries.

Acceptance Criteria:
- No deadlock/livelock in deterministic contention harness.
- Behavior matches defined transition contract and expected errno semantics.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests: uncontended, contended, spurious wake, invalid ownership, unlock-not-owner.
- Stress tests: high-contention N-thread lock/unlock loops.
- Structured logs: trace_id, mode, thread_id, op, futex_branch, result, errno, timing.

Extreme Optimization Hook:
- Capture baseline/profile for lock/unlock hot path and document first opportunity matrix.","notes":"Kickoff progress (RusticCastle):

- Claimed bead and aligned scope with coordination thread.
- Reproduced existing mutex-core test hang baseline:
  - `timeout 20s cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_mutex_core_test -- --nocapture`
  - result: timeout (exit 124) after launching test binary with no case-level output.
- This confirms current deterministic contention harness instability remains active and should be the first stabilization target for bd-19j.

Planned next steps:
1) instrument pthread_abi mutex fast/slow path entry/exit points with deterministic trace counters;
2) isolate whether stall is pre-test synchronization vs futex wait/wake path;
3) add focused adversarial tests for spurious wake / ownership edge cases and emit structured futex-branch logs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RusticCastle","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:51.055961298Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T06:25:38.190421780Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T06:25:38.190266059Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","futex","implementation","pthread","testing"],"comments":[{"id":656,"issue_id":"bd-19j","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Resolved deterministic pthread mutex integration-test hang by hardening test/runtime boundaries. Main changes: debug builds now gate ABI exports with cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle)); build.rs now applies libc version script only when debug assertions are off; pthread mutex integration test uses AtomicBool serialization guard; allocator/string/stdlib/time paths include reentrancy-safe fallbacks; frankenlibc-core math uses libm to avoid recursive symbol interception. Verification passed: cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_mutex_core_test -- --nocapture --test-threads=1 (4 passed), cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --lib (8 passed), cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --release.","created_at":"2026-02-13T06:25:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-19r","title":"Kernel: SOS barrier certificates (runtime polynomial evaluator)","description":"Implement runtime evaluator for SOS barrier polynomials.

Requirements:
- Fixed-point coefficients and inputs.
- Horner-style evaluation; constant-time wrt degree.
- No allocations; minimal branching.

Deliverables:
- sos_barrier.rs (or barrier_cert.rs) + tests for numeric stability on representative ranges.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleOwl","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:32:26.230148859Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T18:44:55.053514094Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T18:44:55.053486502Z","close_reason":"Implemented sos_barrier.rs: 2 barrier certificates (provenance hot-path <15ns, quarantine cadence-gated <100ns), fixed-point arithmetic, SosBarrierController state machine, 23 tests all passing, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-19r","depends_on_id":"bd-2pw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-19r","depends_on_id":"bd-gn9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1ah8","title":"bd-qwm subtask: LD_PRELOAD startup non-regression smoke coverage with failure signatures","description":"Background:
- Startup work must translate into real-world stability in smoke and integration suites.

Goal:
- Validate startup non-regression across LD_PRELOAD smoke scenarios and integration C fixtures.

Deliverables:
1) Startup-focused smoke suite segment and diagnostics.
2) Regression guard for known startup hang/crash signatures.
3) Operational troubleshooting notes driven by evidence logs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- No startup regressions introduced in smoke/integration gates.
- Startup failures are diagnosable without manual instrumentation.

Verification & Logging:
- E2E smoke scripts and integration checks with startup lens.
- Structured logs: trace_id, workload, startup_path, failure_signature, timing, artifact_refs.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:53.698663169Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T06:12:34.922427516Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","diagnostics","e2e","startup","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ah8","depends_on_id":"bd-3bvo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ah8","depends_on_id":"bd-ahjd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1az","title":"Drift: RuntimeKernelSnapshot schema versioning + stability policy","description":"Make snapshot schema stable and future-proof.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add an explicit schema version field.
- Policy: additive-only changes; removals require version bump and migration plan.
- Document each snapshot field: what it means, units, expected range.

Rationale:
- Snapshots are fixtures for regression detection and harness diffs.","notes":"Implemented RuntimeKernelSnapshot schema stability: added schema version field + constant, documented additive-only policy, and generated a complete field schema doc.

Key files:
- crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs (schema_version + RUNTIME_KERNEL_SNAPSHOT_SCHEMA_VERSION)
- crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/runtime_kernel_snapshot_schema.md (116 fields: meaning/units/range)
- crates/glibc-rs-harness/src/snapshot_diff.rs (include schema_version in key diff fields)
- tests/runtime_math/golden/kernel_snapshot_smoke.v1.json + tests/runtime_math/golden/sha256sums.txt updated via scripts/update_golden_snapshots.sh","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltForge","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:36:14.020301546Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T06:57:04.971345778Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T06:57:04.971273723Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1d6e","title":"Seqlocks for configuration hot paths (section 14.9)","description":"Implement seqlocks (sequence locks) for configuration hot paths in FrankenLibC. A seqlock uses a sequence counter: writers increment before and after updating. Readers read the counter, read the data, read the counter again — if the counter changed or is odd, retry. Seqlocks are optimal when: writes are rare, reads are extremely frequent, and the data is small (fits in a few cache lines). Unlike RW locks, readers never block writers. Application to FrankenLibC: (1) Allocator tuning parameters — arena count, size-class boundaries, fragmentation thresholds. Read on every allocation decision, updated only by explicit reconfiguration. (2) TSM sensitivity thresholds — the boundary between 'safe' and 'suspicious' states. Read on every TSM check, updated only by policy changes. (3) Clock configuration — vDSO-style time source configuration. Read on every clock_gettime, updated only on clock source changes. (4) Locale state — the active locale affects string comparison, character classification. Read by every locale-sensitive function, updated only by setlocale. Implementation: SeqLock<T: Copy> — requires T to be Copy (readers may see partially-updated data and must be able to discard it). For non-Copy types, use SeqLockRef<T> with retry-on-torn-read semantics. Performance target: reader fast path <3ns (load seq, load data, load seq, compare). Writer: <10ns (store seq+1, store data, store seq+2, fence).

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Section 14.9 of graveyard. Seqlocks from Lameter (2005). Linux kernel seqlock implementation. Optimized for rare-write, frequent-read small data.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- SeqLock<T: Copy>: wraps T with sequence counter. seq: AtomicU32 (even = stable, odd = write in progress).
- read() -> T: loop { let s1 = seq.load(Acquire); let val = data.read(); let s2 = seq.load(Acquire); if s1 == s2 && s1 % 2 == 0 { return val; } spin_loop(); }
- write(val: T): seq.fetch_add(1, Release); data.write(val); seq.fetch_add(1, Release); fence(SeqCst);
- SeqLockRef<T> for non-Copy: retry-on-torn-read semantics using checksum or version comparison.
- For large T: split into SeqLock<[u8; N]> with N = size_of::<T>() and manual serialization.
- Writer serialization: external Mutex for multi-writer scenarios (seqlock itself only serializes reads vs writes).","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
1. SeqLock<u64> read latency <3ns at p99 (load-load-load-compare, no RMW).
2. Writer latency <10ns including both counter increments and fence.
3. Torn-read detection: injected partial write detected and retried (never returns inconsistent data).
4. 16-reader + 1-writer stress test: 1M operations, zero torn reads, TSan clean.
5. Configuration hot path: allocator tuning params readable in <3ns vs ~15ns with mutex.
6. Works correctly for T up to 64 bytes (covers all configuration structs).
7. Retry rate <1% under normal write frequency (1 write per 10K reads).

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- tracing::trace!(target: seqlock, operation=read, retries, seq_value) per read (disabled in release).
- tracing::debug!(target: seqlock, operation=write, seq_before, seq_after) per write.
- Periodic: avg_retry_rate, max_retries_single_read via tracing::info every 100K ops.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CloudyCoast","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:29:58.086466645Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T21:07:31.752288101Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1d6e","depends_on_id":"bd-3aof","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":657,"issue_id":"bd-1d6e","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Card 3 (seqlock config path) applies; include loom validation + contention benchmark baseline comparator.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:28:49Z"},{"id":1242,"issue_id":"bd-1d6e","author":"CloudyCoast","text":"Progress slice (CloudyCoast): implemented `SeqLock<T: Copy>` in `crates/frankenlibc-core/src/rcu/mod.rs` with <=64-byte payload contract and atomic u64 lane storage to avoid torn snapshots.

Added APIs: `new`, `read`, `read_with_retries`, `write`, `update`, `sequence`, plus `Default`.

Added unit tests:
- roundtrip
- update composition
- >64-byte payload rejection
- concurrent read/write checksum consistency (no torn reads observed)

Validation (all via `rch`):
- `cargo fmt --check` PASS
- `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` PASS
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-core rcu -- --nocapture` PASS (27 tests)
- `cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-core --all-targets -- -D warnings` PASS

Workspace clippy still has pre-existing unrelated failures in `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/sos_barrier.rs` (`needless_range_loop`).
","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:07:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1es","title":"RaptorQ Runtime: Deterministic repair symbol generation (overhead model + R derivation)","description":"Implement or specify deterministic repair symbol generation for evidence objects.

Spec-derived formula to adopt:
- slack_decode = 2 (target K+2 decode slack)
- R = max(slack_decode, ceil(K_source * overhead_percent / 100))
- loss_fraction_max approx (R - slack_decode)/(K_source + R)

Constraints:
- Generation MUST NOT run per call; run on epoch boundary/cadence.
- Seed must be deterministic from epoch/object id.

Deliverables:
- A minimal encoder interface and test vectors.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GrayPond","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:34:56.148303508Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:34:50.848397590Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:34:28.850099524Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1es","depends_on_id":"bd-3a9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1es","depends_on_id":"bd-kom","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":658,"issue_id":"bd-1es","author":"FuchsiaHollow","text":"Verified complete. All deliverables present in evidence.rs. 9/9 tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-11T01:34:50Z"}]}

@@ -325,6 +325,35 @@ else
 fi
 echo ""
 
+echo "--- Check 10: Startup smoke diagnostics contract ---"
+startup_contract_fail=0
+ld_smoke_script="${ROOT}/scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh"
+if [[ ! -f "${ld_smoke_script}" ]]; then
+    echo "FAIL: scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh not found"
+    startup_contract_fail=1
+else
+    for marker in \
+        "FAILURE_SIGNATURE_DENYLIST" \
+        "signature_guard_triggered" \
+        "startup_troubleshooting.md" \
+        "\"startup_path\"" \
+        "\"failure_signature\"" \
+        "classify_failure_signature" \
+        "case_startup_path"; do
+        if ! grep -Fq "${marker}" "${ld_smoke_script}"; then
+            echo "  missing marker in ld_preload_smoke.sh: ${marker}"
+            startup_contract_fail=1
+        fi
+    done
+fi
+if [[ "${startup_contract_fail}" -ne 0 ]]; then
+    echo "FAIL: startup smoke diagnostics contract regression"
+    failures=$((failures + 1))
+else
+    echo "PASS: startup smoke diagnostics contract markers present"
+fi
+echo ""
+
 echo "=== Summary ==="
 echo "Failures: ${failures}"
 echo "Note: interpose-stage functional failures are expected; this gate validates deterministic E2E infrastructure and policy reporting."

@@ -20,6 +20,7 @@ TRACE_FILE="${RUN_DIR}/trace.jsonl"
 CASE_TSV="${RUN_DIR}/abi_compat_cases.tsv"
 REPORT_FILE="${RUN_DIR}/abi_compat_report.json"
 TROUBLESHOOT_FILE="${RUN_DIR}/startup_troubleshooting.md"
+BEAD_ID="${BEAD_ID:-bd-1ah8}"
 FAILURE_SIGNATURE_DENYLIST="${FAILURE_SIGNATURE_DENYLIST:-startup_timeout,startup_segv,startup_abort,startup_symbol_lookup_error,startup_loader_missing_library,startup_glibc_version_mismatch,startup_elf_class_mismatch}"
 
 LIB_CANDIDATES=(
@@ -236,12 +237,13 @@ emit_trace() {
   local ts
   ts="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
 
-  local trace_id="bd-18qq.6::${RUN_ID}::${mode}::${label}"
-  printf '{"timestamp":"%s","trace_id":"%s","level":"%s","event":"%s","bead_id":"bd-18qq.6","run_id":"%s","mode":"%s","case":"%s","status":"%s","api_family":"abi-interposition","symbol":"%s","decision_path":"%s","healing_action":"%s","errno":%s,"latency_ns":%s,"artifact_refs":%s,"workload":"%s","startup_path":"%s","failure_signature":"%s","timing":%s%s}\n' \
+  local trace_id="${BEAD_ID}::${RUN_ID}::${mode}::${label}"
+  printf '{"timestamp":"%s","trace_id":"%s","level":"%s","event":"%s","bead_id":"%s","run_id":"%s","mode":"%s","case":"%s","status":"%s","api_family":"abi-interposition","symbol":"%s","decision_path":"%s","healing_action":"%s","errno":%s,"latency_ns":%s,"artifact_refs":%s,"workload":"%s","startup_path":"%s","failure_signature":"%s","timing":%s%s}\n' \
     "${ts}" \
     "${trace_id}" \
     "${level}" \
     "${event}" \
+    "${BEAD_ID}" \
     "${RUN_ID}" \
     "${mode}" \
     "${label}" \
@@ -264,24 +266,32 @@ append_case_row() {
   local mode="$1"
   local label="$2"
   local status="$3"
-  local parity_required="$4"
-  local parity_pass="$5"
-  local perf_required="$6"
-  local perf_pass="$7"
-  local latency_ratio_ppm="$8"
-  local baseline_rc="$9"
-  local preload_rc="${10}"
-  local stdout_match="${11}"
-  local stderr_match="${12}"
-  local baseline_latency_ns="${13}"
-  local preload_latency_ns="${14}"
-  local valgrind_checked="${15}"
-  local valgrind_pass="${16}"
-
-  printf '%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\n' \
+  local workload="$4"
+  local startup_path="$5"
+  local failure_signature="$6"
+  local signature_guard_triggered="$7"
+  local parity_required="$8"
+  local parity_pass="$9"
+  local perf_required="${10}"
+  local perf_pass="${11}"
+  local latency_ratio_ppm="${12}"
+  local baseline_rc="${13}"
